message:
feat(runtime): add opt-out signal interception and serialize shutdown tests

Adds a new `intercept_signals: bool` field to `ProgramConfig`
(defaulting to `true` so production behavior is unchanged) and a
`with_signal_interception` builder. The field is propagated through
every `Program` constructor — `CrosstermEventSource` / `Stdout`,
generic backend, and the headless test backend — into a matching
`intercept_signals` field on the `Program` struct.

All six `check_termination_signal()` sites in `run_event_loop` and
`run` are replaced with calls to a new `#[inline]
observed_termination_signal(&self)` helper that returns `None` when
`intercept_signals` is false. The hot path is unchanged for
production use (inlined away entirely) but tests can now construct
`Program` instances that ignore the process-global signal slot,
which eliminates an entire class of inter-test interference.

## Why

The runtime previously observed `shutdown_signal::check_termination_signal()`
unconditionally on every loop iteration. This is correct for real
TUI programs — Ctrl-C / SIGTERM must stop the event loop cleanly —
but is hostile to unit tests that do NOT want the test harness's
process-global signal state leaking into their runtime. For
example, a completely unrelated test that records a pending
termination signal can cause every subsequent headless runtime test
to abort on its first loop iteration.

## Test harness updates

`headless_program_with_config` (the default test constructor) now
delegates to `headless_program_with_resolved_config` after calling
`.with_signal_interception(false)`, so every existing test
automatically gets the safe behavior without edits. A new
`headless_signal_program_with_config` preserves the old
signal-observing behavior and is used by exactly the two tests that
*need* to exercise signal handling:

- the SIGINT-style shutdown test that records signal 2 and asserts
  `shutdowns == 1`
- the SIGTERM-style early-stop test that records signal 15 before
  the first render and asserts render/subscription counts are zero

Both of those tests are additionally wrapped in
`ftui_core::shutdown_signal::with_test_signal_serialization(|| { .. })`
(introduced in the preceding ftui-core commit) so they can never
race with each other or with any other signal-sensitive test in any
other workspace crate.
